notes: NI Scarbee A-200 — deeply sampled Wurlitzer A-200 electric piano in Kontakt. The Wurlitzer uses metal reeds and electrostatic pickups rather than the Rhodes' tines — producing its characteristic buzzy, nasal, slightly gritty tone. Overdrive sits naturally in the Wurlitzer signal chain; even modest drive amounts (QC5 around 40–60) give the classic Supertramp/Doors sound. Tremolo is built-in. The reed character (QC1) controls how much the electrostatic buzz and bite comes through — essential for period-correct tone. Crosstalk (QC2) simulates the subtle bleed between adjacent reed/pickup pairs that defines the Wurlitzer's unique harmonic complexity.

Soft Pedal.description: Soft pedal — reduces attack and softens tone

Quick Control 1.description: Reed Character / Bite — electrostatic buzz and nasal bite of the reed; high = more aggressive 'wasp' tone, low = smoother and cleaner

Quick Control 2.description: Crosstalk — inter-reed capacitive bleed; adds the warm, complex harmonic shimmer unique to Wurlitzer (keep between 20–60 for realism)

Quick Control 3.description: Tremolo Rate — speed of built-in stereo tremolo

Quick Control 4.description: Tremolo Depth — stereo tremolo swing width (0 = off)

Quick Control 5.description: Overdrive Amount — built-in overdrive; essential for classic Wurlitzer tone. 30–60 = vintage breakup, 80+ = heavy Doors-style grind

Quick Control 6.description: Release Noise — reed damper noise on key release

Quick Control 7.description: Mechanical Noise — key action and body mechanical noise level

Quick Control 8.description: Humanise — random velocity and tuning variation for a more human, less perfect feel
